---
title: "Session Constraints"
---

## Overview

`dl::constraints` ([`ConstraintEnforcer.hpp`](../../../../src/DeepLearning/constraints/ConstraintEnforcer.hpp),
[`ConstraintEnforcer.cpp`](../../../../src/DeepLearning/constraints/ConstraintEnforcer.cpp))
provides pure, Qt-free functions that derive allowed batch sizes and decoder
alternatives from `ModelInfo` plus session binding configuration. Shared by
the Deep Learning Properties widget and future command-interface inference paths.

## API

```cpp
namespace dl::constraints {

struct BatchSizeConstraint {
    int min = 1;
    int max = 0;              // 0 = unlimited
    bool forced_by_recurrent = false;
};

BatchSizeConstraint computeBatchSizeConstraint(
    dl::ModelInfo const& info,
    std::vector<dl::MemoryFrameBinding> const& memory_frames);

std::vector<std::string> validDecodersForPostEncoder(
    dl::PostEncoderStepDescriptor const& params);

} // namespace dl::constraints
```

## Batch-size constraint

`computeBatchSizeConstraint()` reads `info.batch_mode` and checks
`hasActiveRecurrentBindings(memory_frames)`.

A recurrent binding is **active** when `hasActiveRecurrentBinding()` is true:
`output_slot_name` is non-empty and not `"(None)"` (see
`DeepLearningBindingData.hpp`).

Implementation logic:

1. If **any active recurrent binding** OR **`isBatchLocked(batch_mode)`**
   (`RecurrentOnlyBatch` or `FixedBatch{1}`) → return `{1, 1, has_recurrent}`.
   `forced_by_recurrent` is `true` only when recurrent bindings caused the lock.
2. Else if **`FixedBatch{N>1}`** → `{N, N, false}`.
3. Else **`DynamicBatch`** → `{minBatchSizeFromMode, maxBatchSizeFromMode, false}`.

Priority table (most restrictive wins):

| Condition | `min` | `max` | `forced_by_recurrent` |
|-----------|-------|-------|------------------------|
| Active recurrent bindings | 1 | 1 | `true` |
| `RecurrentOnlyBatch` or `FixedBatch{1}` | 1 | 1 | `false` |
| `FixedBatch{N>1}` | N | N | `false` |
| `DynamicBatch{min, max}` | `min` | `max` (`0` = unlimited) | `false` |

When both active recurrent bindings and `FixedBatch{N>1}` apply, recurrent
wins (batch locked to 1 with `forced_by_recurrent=true`).

### Widget usage

`DeepLearningPropertiesWidget::_updateBatchSizeConstraint()` maps the result to
the batch spinbox (range, enabled state, tooltips). See
[Batch Mode Guide](batch_mode_guide.qmd) for model-level `BatchMode` semantics.

## Post-encoder / decoder consistency

`validDecodersForPostEncoder(params)` queries
`PostEncoderModuleRegistry::collapsesSpatialDims(params.module_key)`.

| Post-encoder | Allowed decoders |
|--------------|------------------|
| `module_key == "none"` | All four: `MaskDecoderParams`, `PointDecoderParams`, `LineDecoderParams`, `FeatureVectorDecoderParams` |
| Module collapses `[B,C,H,W]` → `[B,C]` (e.g. `global_avg_pool`) | `FeatureVectorDecoderParams` only |

Returned strings are reflect-cpp alternative names for
`AutoParamWidget::updateVariantAlternatives()`.

`DeepLearningPropertiesWidget::_enforcePostEncoderDecoderConsistency()` calls
this after post-encoder changes and filters each `OutputSlotWidget`'s decoder
combo. `SlotAssembler` rank guards provide a second line of defense for
misconfigured sessions.

See [Post-encoder modules](post_encoder_modules.qmd) for registry metadata.

## Consumers

| Consumer | Usage |
|----------|-------|
| `DeepLearningPropertiesWidget` | `_updateBatchSizeConstraint()` — spinbox; `_enforcePostEncoderDecoderConsistency()` — output decoder filtering |
| Command interface (planned) | Pre-run session validation |

## Tests

`tests/DeepLearning/constraints/ConstraintEnforcer.test.cpp`

| Tag | Coverage |
|-----|----------|
| `[dl_constraints]` | `computeBatchSizeConstraint` across batch modes and recurrent binding states |
| `[dl_constraints][decoder_compat]` | `validDecodersForPostEncoder` for `"none"` vs spatial-collapsing modules |

## See also

- [Batch Mode Guide](batch_mode_guide.qmd) — `BatchMode`, widget spinbox mapping
- [ModelInfo](models_v2/ModelInfo.qmd) — `batch_mode` field source
- [Memory frame bindings](bindings/memory_frames.qmd) — recurrent vs static frames
